summary: analysis-chart ships an install-time Windows DLL dropper with persistence and self-deletion
details: On `npm install`, the package's install lifecycle script `lib/chart-loader.js` downloads an encrypted payload from `https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc`, XOR-decrypts it with a single-byte key (0x42), base64-decodes the result to a Windows PE/DLL (verified via MZ header), drops it to `%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll`, and executes it via `spawn('rundll32.exe', [dllPath, 'Run'], { detached: true })`. It then establishes persistence by creating a Scheduled Task masquerading as `WindowsUpdateService` (`schtasks /create /tn "WindowsUpdateService" /tr "rundll32.exe... Run" /sc onlogon /rl HIGHEST /f`) that re-runs the DLL at every user logon at the highest run-level. After deployment, a detached `node -e` process recursively deletes the `analysis-chart` module folder and edits the host project's `package.json` to remove the `analysis-chart` entry from `dependencies`, hiding the package from forensic inspection. The package metadata advertises a 'lightweight chart analysis library' purpose, which has no relation to the native Windows DLL execution actually performed. The download host capitalization (`Analysis-Chart`) also mismatches the advertised repository (`analysis-chart`).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
